entries.name: ribbon-icon
entries.purpose: Shows where Agent Console lives — the ribbon entry point.
entries.caption: Open the agent from the ribbon
entries.altText: Agent Console ribbon icon with its tooltip in the Obsidian left sidebar

entries.name: multi-session
entries.purpose: Run several agent sessions side by side, each tab showing its own status, with rich Obsidian-rendered replies.
entries.caption: See every agent at a glance
entries.altText: Agent Console sidebar with multiple session tabs each showing a status icon, beside a Bases dashboard and a rich agent reply
entries.followUp: Tab status indicators (.agent-client-tab-state-icon) are not lit up: only the active tab reconnects (reconnectActive), so the inactive restored tabs show unlit/idle status glyphs. Next cycle: reconnect all restored tabs (or seed a lit status) so every tab's state icon reads as connected.

entries.name: mode-selection
entries.purpose: Pick how much autonomy the agent gets, per session.
entries.caption: Control what the agent can do
entries.altText: Agent Console permission-mode menu showing Default, Accept Edits, Plan Mode, Don't Ask, and Bypass Permissions

entries.name: model-selection
entries.purpose: Switch the model the agent runs on.
entries.caption: Choose your model
entries.altText: Agent Console model menu listing the available models for the session

entries.name: thought-level-selection
entries.purpose: Tune how much reasoning effort the agent spends per session.
entries.caption: Dial the agent’s effort up or down
entries.altText: Agent Console effort-level menu showing Default, Low, Medium, High, and Max

entries.name: switch-agent
entries.purpose: Switch the active session to a different agent.
entries.caption: Switch between agents
entries.altText: Agent Console More menu open with the Switch agent option

entries.name: export
entries.purpose: Export a chat to a Markdown note.
entries.caption: Save any chat as a note
entries.altText: Export chat to Markdown button in the Agent Console chat header, with its tooltip

entries.name: sending-messages
entries.draftMessage: What can Agent Console do with my notes?
entries.purpose: Chat with an agent about the note you're in.
entries.caption: Pin notes into the chat
entries.altText: Agent Console composer with the Welcome note pinned in the context strip, ready to chat

entries.name: switch-default-agent
entries.purpose: Pick the agent every new chat opens with.
entries.caption: Set your default agent
entries.altText: Agent Console settings with the Default agent dropdown open, listing the available agents

entries.name: editing
entries.purpose: Approve or reject a file edit the agent proposes, with a full diff.
entries.caption: Approve edits before they happen
entries.altText: Agent Console permission card showing a proposed file edit diff with Allow and Reject buttons

entries.name: slash-commands-1
entries.purpose: Discover and run agent slash commands from the composer.
entries.caption: Run slash commands inline
entries.altText: Agent Console composer slash-command menu listing the available commands

entries.name: slash-commands-2
entries.purpose: Filter slash commands as you type.
entries.caption: Filter commands as you type
entries.altText: Agent Console slash-command menu filtered to the extensions commands as the user types

entries.name: sending-images
entries.purpose: Attach an image to the chat and send it to the agent.
entries.caption: Drop in images and files
entries.altText: Agent Console composer with an image thumbnail attached below the message box, ready to send

entries.name: remove-image
entries.purpose: Remove an attached image before sending.
entries.caption: Remove an attachment in one click
entries.altText: Close-up of an attached image thumbnail with its remove button in the composer

entries.name: temporary-disable
entries.purpose: Pin or unpin the active note as chat context.
entries.caption: Control what the agent sees
entries.altText: Removing the auto-pinned active note from the context strip, then pinning it back with the grab button

entries.name: mention-context
entries.purpose: Mention notes with @ to build the chat's context.
entries.caption: Pull notes into context with @
entries.altText: Typing an @ note mention, choosing a note, and sending — the mentioned notes appear as pills in the context strip

entries.name: parallel-sessions
entries.purpose: Run multiple agent sessions in parallel tabs, each with its own status.
entries.caption: Run agents in parallel tabs
entries.altText: Two agent session tabs side by side — switching between them shows each keeps its own conversation and status icon

entries.name: multi-session-animated
entries.purpose: Run several agent sessions in parallel tabs, cycling the active tab to show each keeps its own conversation and status.
entries.caption: Run agents in parallel tabs
entries.altText: Agent Console cycling three parallel session tabs — Japan trip, Launch check, Newsletter — each with its own conversation and status

entries.name: tab-status-dropdown
entries.purpose: Per-tab status glyphs in the tab bar — spot the active, idle, or stuck session at a glance.
entries.differentiator: Per-tab status glyphs in the tab-list dropdown
entries.caption: See every session's status at a glance
entries.altText: Agent Console tab-list dropdown: five sessions, each with a status glyph for ready, busy, awaiting permission, errored, and disconnected.
entries.captureNote: Driven by initialState.forceTabStates: seeds 5 nicely-labeled tabs forced into the five visual states with nativeMenus off (so the chevron opens a DOM .menu), then clickSelector opens the chevron. Window-mode, dpr-independent — no screen-mode crop.

entries.name: getting-started
entries.purpose: First-run panel that lists detected agents to pick from instead of leaving a dead end.
entries.caption: Start in one click on a detected agent
entries.altText: Agent Console getting-started panel showing detected agents as one-click picks and an Open settings button
entries.captureNote: Requires a no-connectable-agent state (default a built-in not on PATH); fixtures vault has agents configured, so needs a temporary mis-config or dedicated fixture profile.

entries.name: command-palette
entries.purpose: The cold-start command palette: Agent Console's top-level commands — new/open chat, open new view, the agent picker, quick prompts, and settings import.
entries.caption: A palette you can actually scan
entries.altText: Obsidian command palette filtered to Agent Console, showing New chat, Open chat, Open new view, New chat with agent, and Quick prompts

entries.name: new-chat-agent-picker
entries.purpose: The single New chat with agent picker that replaced the per-agent commands.
entries.caption: Pick an agent for a fresh chat
entries.altText: Agent Console New chat with agent fuzzy picker listing the configured agents
entries.captureNote: FuzzySuggestModal launched by running the 'New chat with agent' command; needs command execution the pipeline can't drive yet -- capture manually.

entries.name: settings-import
entries.purpose: Import agent definitions, defaults, and keys from another agent plugin on first run.
entries.caption: Bring your config over in one step
entries.altText: Agent Console settings-import preview modal listing the agents and keys that would be imported
entries.captureNote: Requires a second agent plugin (e.g. Agent Client) installed so there is something to import; trigger the import modal from settings.

entries.name: update-pill
entries.purpose: The header pill that opens Community plugins when an update is available.
entries.caption: One click to update
entries.altText: The Agent Console chat header with the Plugin update available pill
entries.captureNote: Pill only renders when an update is available; needs a mocked update-available state to capture.

entries.name: session-history-search
entries.purpose: Full-text search across your local session library from the history modal.
entries.caption: Find any past chat by title or content
entries.altText: Agent Console session-history modal with a search box filtering sessions, showing highlighted title and snippet matches
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PR #89. Confirm the search-input + result-snippet selectors and final dims at capture; needs seeded fixture sessions with searchable content.

entries.name: queue-next-message
entries.purpose: Queue one next message while the agent is still streaming; it auto-sends when the turn returns to ready.
entries.caption: Line up your next message without waiting
entries.altText: Agent Console composer locked with a 'Queued — sends when ready' banner and Edit and Delete controls, holding the next message until ready
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PR #97. Confirm the queued-message element selector + final dims at capture; requires driving a session into streaming state with a queued message.

entries.name: mid-stream-steering
entries.purpose: Redirect the agent mid-reply — one keypress stops the current reply and sends your message instead of waiting for the turn to finish.
entries.caption: Redirect the agent the moment it goes sideways
entries.altText: Agent Console composer while the agent is replying, showing the hint to press Enter to queue or Mod+Enter to send now
entries.captureNote: v2.1.0. PENDING — capture the streaming composer showing the queue/steer keybinding hint (empty composer while streaming), or the steering banner. Confirm the selector + final dims at capture; requires driving a session into streaming state.

entries.name: shared-links-bubble
entries.purpose: See and reopen the links shared during a session from a bubble in the chat header.
entries.caption: Jump back to links shared in the chat
entries.altText: Agent Console shared-links bubble with an accent count badge and its open dropdown grouping links into New this session and Earlier
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PR #103 (focus ring PR #116). Confirm the shared-links bubble selector and final dims at capture; drive a session that has shared at least one link so the bubble renders.

entries.name: ai-session-titles
entries.purpose: Chat tabs are labeled with concise AI-generated titles derived from each session's content.
entries.caption: Tabs name themselves from the conversation
entries.altText: Agent Console tab bar with chat tabs showing concise AI-generated session titles instead of generic labels
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PR #119. Confirm the tab-label selector and final dims at capture; needs seeded sessions whose content yields meaningful generated titles.

entries.name: first-run-activation
entries.purpose: Brand-new users with no agent CLI get an actionable install panel instead of a dead-end.
entries.caption: Get started even when no agent is installed yet
entries.altText: Agent Console first-run panel with no agent installed, listing Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI and Kiro CLI with Install and Copy buttons
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 first-run-activation PR. Needs the no-agent state: deploy the fixtures studio with smoke-test-spawn.sh --first-run --no-agents (or drop the .force-no-agents marker), open a new chat. Confirm the .agent-client-getting-started crop selector + final dims at capture.

entries.name: obsidian-system-prompt
entries.purpose: An editable Obsidian system prompt the agent receives on its first message, with per-block toggles and reset to defaults.
entries.caption: Tell the agent it runs in Obsidian
entries.altText: Agent Console settings showing the editable Obsidian system prompt with per-block include toggles and a reset-to-defaults button
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PRs #168/#170 (shipped as Obsidian System Prompt). Confirm the Obsidian-system-prompt settings-section selector + final dims at capture; scroll the settings pane to that accordion first.

entries.name: collapsible-agent-sections
entries.purpose: Built-in agent settings collapse into accordions so the pane stays scannable.
entries.caption: Keep the settings pane tidy
entries.altText: Agent Console settings with each built-in agent shown as a collapsed accordion section under Built-in agents
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PR #104. collapseSelectors collapses every .agent-client-agent-section; scrollToSettingText leads with the Built-in agents heading.

entries.name: settings-working-directory
entries.purpose: Set the folder new chats start in — a global default, overridable per agent.
entries.caption: Choose where chats run
entries.altText: Agent Console settings showing the Default working directory option that sets where new chats start
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 PR #100/#154. scrollToSettingText centers the Default working directory setting-item.

entries.name: quick-prompts
entries.purpose: Reusable saved prompts as chips above the composer and a ! search launcher.
entries.caption: Reuse your favorite prompts
entries.altText: Agent Console composer with quick-prompt chips above the box and the ! launcher dropdown open: prompts, a Create row, and a key legend
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 quick-prompts (#130/#144/#150/#152/#161/#169). Seeded Quick Prompts/ fixture notes (3 always-show chips: a {{selection}} chip, a new-tab chip, a plain one; plus search-only prompts). No agent connection needed (composer is live pre-connect); draftMessage ! opens the launcher; union-crops chip bar + ! dropdown + composer box.

entries.name: confirm-close-multiple-tabs
entries.purpose: Warn before a focused Cmd+W tears down a panel holding multiple live chats.
entries.caption: Don't lose chats to one keystroke
entries.altText: Agent Console confirm-close dialog warning that closing the panel closes all open chats, with Cancel and Close panel buttons
entries.captureNote: v1.3.0 #84. forceTabStates seeds 2 ready tabs; forceCloseConfirm invokes the panel close handler so the real ConfirmCloseModal opens; window-mode .modal crop.

entries.name: editor-quickprompts
entries.differentiator: Grounded quick prompts on your notes
entries.caption: One-tap prompts, grounded in the note you're on
entries.purpose: Kick off common asks — summarize, explain, draft — against the note you're viewing, without typing a prompt.
entries.altText: Agent Console beside a Weekly review note: tabbed sessions, a meeting-prep chat, and the quick-prompt menu open with one-tap prompts
entries.followUp: Tab status indicators (.agent-client-tab-state-icon) are not lit up: only the active tab reconnects (reconnectActive), so the inactive restored tabs show unlit/idle status glyphs. Next cycle: reconnect all restored tabs (or seed a lit status) so every tab's state icon reads as connected.

entries.name: reload-control
entries.purpose: The header reload control: click to reload the session (keep the conversation), Shift-click to restart fresh.
entries.altText: Agent Console chat header with the reload button among the header actions

entries.name: agent-settings-section
entries.purpose: The Claude Code settings section: agent path, Auto-detect, and API key.
entries.altText: Agent Console settings showing the Claude Code section with API key, path, and Auto-detect controls

entries.name: custom-agents-form
entries.purpose: The Add-custom-agent form: Agent ID, Display name, Path, Arguments, and environment variables.
entries.altText: Agent Console settings showing the Add custom agent form with Agent ID, Display name, and Path fields

entries.name: zero-tab-landing
entries.purpose: Closing every tab lands on a neutral launcher — a live composer, quick prompts, and actions — instead of being blocked.
entries.differentiator: Close last tab to a blank landing (new-tab-page model)
entries.caption: Close everything to a blank slate
entries.altText: Agent Console zero-tab landing: a launcher composer, quick-prompt chips, and New chat / with an agent / Open session history actions
entries.captureNote: Close all tabs (or start with none) so ChatView renders ZeroTabLanding; crop .agent-client-zero-tab-landing. Confirm final dims at capture. Registered pending 2026-07-09.
